Megatron-LM@3b3476116d97.mechanism: Two redundant-work regressions in DynamicInferenceRequest construction/serialization. (1) __post_init__ set `remaining_prompt_tokens = copy.deepcopy(self.prompt_tokens)`, deep-copying the prompt token tensor on every request; the fix replaces it with a direct reference assignment. (2) On record `merge()`, `_compute_block_hashes()` was re-run because the merged request did not carry over prefix-caching fields; the fix propagates `block_size_tokens`, `enable_prefix_caching`, and `precomputed_block_hashes` through merge and adds a `not self.precomputed_block_hashes` guard so the GPU matmul + GPU->CPU `.tolist()` hash computation (compute_block_hashes_batched) is skipped when hashes already exist.

manifest_conditions.hardware: GPU (block-hash path uses CUDA matmul + GPU->CPU transfer)
manifest_conditions.shape: impact scales with prompt token length (deepcopy cost) and number of complete blocks (hash recompute)
manifest_conditions.feature: dynamic inference engine; prefix caching (enable_prefix_caching) and chunked prefill

vllm@506ad7d7c178.mechanism: In `load_model`, the code was `with self._maybe_get_memory_pool_context(tag="weights") and set_current_vllm_config(self.vllm_config):`. Python's boolean `and` on two context-manager objects evaluates to the right operand (both are truthy), so only `set_current_vllm_config(...)`'s `__enter__`/`__exit__` ran — the CuMemAllocator "weights" memory-pool context was never entered. Consequently model weights were allocated outside the "weights" pool, so `allocator.sleep(offload_tags=("weights",))` during sleep mode level 1 could not offload/free the weight memory. The fix replaces `and` with a parenthesized multi-CM `with (A, B):` so both contexts are properly entered.

manifest_conditions.feature: sleep mode (enable_sleep_mode=True), level-1 sleep weight offloading

vllm@7e67df5570e2.mechanism: The Qwen3-VL dummy-inputs builder generated a profiling video with target_num_frames = get_num_frames_with_most_features(...), which for Qwen3-VL was capped at _MAX_FRAMES_PER_VIDEO = 2048 frames. This produces a single video encoder input whose vision-embedding size is enormous. get_max_video_tokens() also used this 2048-frame count to size the encoder-cache budget. In V1, if a single multimodal item's encoder outputs can never fit the encoder-cache budget, the scheduler cannot make progress and hangs. The fix pins dummy videos to num_frames=2 (target_num_frames = 2; get_max_video_tokens now calls get_num_video_tokens(..., num_frames=2)) and derives the max video-token size from the max-pixel image dimensions (get_image_size_with_most_features(max_pixels=video_max_pixels // temporal_patch_size)) instead of a 2048-frame video, restoring a consistent, schedulable encoder-cache budget.

vllm@bcc2306cefa4.mechanism: The prefetch weight offloader gated pinned-CPU-memory allocation only on is_pin_memory_available(), ignoring the user override VLLM_WEIGHT_OFFLOADING_DISABLE_PIN_MEMORY. On unified-memory systems (GH200) pinned CPU memory is carved out of GPU memory, so users set that env var to avoid the "doubled allocation" it causes; but prefetch offloading kept allocating pinned storage regardless. The fix introduces should_pin_memory() (is_pin_memory_available() AND NOT VLLM_WEIGHT_OFFLOADING_DISABLE_PIN_MEMORY) and routes _CpuParamOffloader's torch.empty_strided pinned allocations and the pinned-copy path through it, so the env var actually reduces GPU memory pressure.

vllm@bfaa5593050e.mechanism: The reverted commit d7982daff changed the fused MoE Triton kernel stride arguments (stride_am, stride_ak, stride_be, ... stride_bbn) from default (int32) to `tl.int64` to avoid an illegal memory access (IMA) from int32 stride overflow. int64 pointer/stride arithmetic on GPU is more expensive (higher register pressure and more instructions per address computation) than int32, so it slows the MoE GEMM kernels. This revert restores the int32 strides, undoing that slowdown for the common case where strides fit in int32.

manifest_conditions.dtype: includes GPTQ/AWQ quantized (fused_moe_kernel_gptq_awq) and block-wise quantized paths
manifest_conditions.shape: matters when M/K/N strides fit within int32 range (no overflow); large tensors would require int64 for correctness (the reverted IMA fix)

vllm@d0cd736caada.mechanism: The multimodal processor and the AsyncMicrobatchTokenizer shared the same underlying HuggingFace Rust fast-tokenizer instance. Under concurrent load, simultaneous access from call_hf_processor and the async tokenizer triggered a Rust RefCell "RuntimeError: Already borrowed", causing request failures/contention that degraded VLM serving throughput. The fix `mm_tokenizer = copy.deepcopy(tokenizer)` gives the mm processor its own tokenizer backend, eliminating the cross-path borrow conflict.

manifest_conditions.tokenizer: HuggingFace fast/Rust-backed tokenizer with internal RefCell
